heroText: Lome is a capital city in Togo, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

highlights.name: Lome
highlights.text: Lome is a capital city in Togo, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

history.label: 19th century
history.text: The city was founded by the Ewes and expanded in the 19th century by German, British and African traders, becoming the capital of Togoland in 1897.
Togoland was thus created as an entity of international law within the German colonial empire on 5 July 1884, by the Treaty of Togoville, signed by Gustav Nachtigal and King Mlapa III.
But, if the infrastructure set up by the Germans - which consisted of a post office in 1890, the telephone in 1894, the cathedral in 1904, a bank in 1906 and the intercontinental telegraph.
Lomé benefited from 1904 from a port that made it the only maritime contact point of Togo, ruining its rival, Aného, until then much more important.
From this development, a network of railways could be deployed: to Aného in 1905, to Kpalimé in 1907 and to Atakpamé in 1909.
Investments stopped, like the Northern Railway, definitively stopped in Blitta in 1934.

history.label: 1970s
history.text: From the mid-1970s, investments became more and more gigantic, but not always in well-targeted areas, Togo, a small open country and hub of trade between its powerful neighbors, did not have the protected market.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The calm and sparsely inhabited Loméen coastline began to be populated rapidly.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: All the "useful Togo" was now organized in a funnel around Lomé, whose preponderance on the Togolese urban network was definitively established and growth assured.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: It is also quite exceptional that an African capital has been so marked by its indigenous bourgeoisie, both in the production practices of urban space, so original in Lomé, and in the singularities of.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The French renewed the infrastructure left by the Germans, repairing railways, building more roads, constructing a new quay, etc.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: It was only after World War II, following a decade of postwar stagnation, that boom times resumed in Lomé. Independence The population of the city increased rapidly in the second half of the 20th.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The city, as throughout the country, the very high prices of export products operate the markets, the significant investments of the colonial administration (the "FIDES" plan created a large number of roads, bridges, schools.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: At the same time, the railways were allowed to deteriorate, even though they had an important role, especially for serving the outlying districts of the city.
